items.title: President Assad reported to have fled Syria after rebels entered capital Damascus
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
President Bashar Assad has left the country for an undisclosed location, according to a Syrian opposition war monitor. The report came shortly after insurgents said they had entered Damascus, capping a stunning advance across the country, as residents of the capital reported sounds of gunfire and explosions.

#syria #assad #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Assad reported to have left Syria as rebels say they entered Damascus
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Syrian opposition fighters said they had entered Damascus following a stunning advance across the country as the head of a Syrian opposition war monitor reported that President Bashar Assad had left the country for an undisclosed location. 

#syria #assad #news

items.title: Thousands of Israelis protest in Tel Aviv before police bring out water cannon
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Police used a water cannon on protestors in Tel Aviv on Saturday as thousands took to the streets for weekly anti-government protests. (AP video shot by Shlomo Mor and produced by Mitya Osipov).

#israel #telaviv #news

items.title: People celebrate with gunfire in Damascus after Syrian insurgents reach capital
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Celebrations erupted in Damascus early on Sunday after insurgents said they had entered the Syrian capital following their advance across the country. (AP video by Abdoulrahman Shaheen).

#syria #assad #news

items.title: Ukrainian children write letters with holiday wishes, dream of peace amid war
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Hopes for victory, a swift end to the military campaign, and prayers for loved ones to survive the violence or return from Russian captivity are among the wishes scattered throughout the letters received by a volunteer group from Ukrainian children living in frontline areas, eagerly awaiting their presents. (AP video by Vasilisa Stepanenko).

#ukraine

items.title: Major damage caused after Syrians storm Iranian embassy in Damascus
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Farming project in South Africa helps deaf people build skills and find jobs
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
At the Westonaria agricultural park near Bekkersdal, east of Johannesburg, farm workers are busy tilling the soil and planting tomatoes and lettuce. Despite the hive of activity, there is silence around the park. Most people here are deaf and communicate using sign language.

#deaf #deafcommunity #southafrica #farming

items.title: People leave Lebanon for Syria after rebels capture Damascus
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
At the Lebanon-Syria border scores of families were returning to Syria Sunday morning, with dozens of cars lining up to enter.

items.title: Syrians in Beirut, Lebanon, react to the fall of Bashar Assad
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Some Syrians in the Lebanese capital, Beirut, expressed joy following the news that the Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family. (AP video by Lujain Jo).

items.title: What to know about fluoride #shorts #health
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Fights are cropping up nationwide over fluoride in drinking water and several communities have decided to stop adding it to water supplies. Here's what to know.

items.title: Syrian army soldiers surrender to rebels after capturing Homs city
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
The key central city of Homs collapsed on Saturday night, and as daylight broke on Sunday opposition fighters were all over the streets of the city celebrating. (AP video: Gaith Alsayed).

#news #syria #damascus #assad

items.title: What does the end of the Assad family’s 50-year rule mean for Syria?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, ending the Assad family’s 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region. 

#news #syria #assad

items.title: Biden says Assad's fall in Syria is a 'fundamental act of justice,' but 'risk'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
U.S. President Joe Biden says the sudden collapse of the Syrian government under Bashar Assad is a “fundamental act of justice” after decades of repression, but it is also “a moment of risk and uncertainty” for the Mideast. 

#syria #biden #news
